Turkey

Partially Funded Australian Awards for Refugee Students in Turkey

Published 10 Jun 20266 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 10 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to Australian Awards for Refugee Students in Turkey

If you are a refugee student in Turkey looking for partially funded scholarship opportunities to pursue your undergraduate or master's degree in Australia, you are in the right place. The Australian Awards are a great way to achieve your academic goals, and we will guide you through the process. The Australian government offers various scholarships to students from different backgrounds, including refugee students in Turkey, to study in Australia.

Who Qualifies

To be eligible for the Australian Awards, you must be a refugee student in Turkey with a valid visa. The following visa categories qualify for the Australian Awards:

* Visa 200 (Refugee)

* Visa 201 (In-country Special Humanitarian)

* Visa 202 (Global Special Humanitarian)

* Visa 203 (Emergency Rescue)

* Visa 204 (Woman at Risk)

Available Scholarships

The Australian government offers various scholarships to refugee students in Turkey. Here are five real scholarships available:

Australia Awards Scholarship

The Australia Awards Scholarship covers up to 100% of tuition fees and living expenses, which is approximately AUD 30,000 per year. To be eligible, you must be a refugee student in Turkey, have a valid visa, and meet the academic requirements of the chosen university. The deadline for application is 30 April 2026. For more information, visit https://www.australiaawards.gov.au/.

Australian Government Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship

The Australian Government RTP Scholarship covers up to 100% of tuition fees and living expenses, which is approximately AUD 28,000 per year. To be eligible, you must be a refugee student in Turkey, have a valid visa, and meet the academic requirements of the chosen university. The deadline for application is 31 October 2026. For more information, visit https://www.education.gov.au/.

Australia Awards Pacific Scholarship

The Australia Awards Pacific Scholarship covers up to 100% of tuition fees and living expenses, which is approximately AUD 25,000 per year. To be eligible, you must be a refugee student in Turkey, have a valid visa, and meet the academic requirements of the chosen university. The deadline for application is 15 June 2026. For more information, visit https://www.australiaawards.gov.au/pacific.

Endeavour Leadership Program

The Endeavour Leadership Program covers up to AUD 25,000 for master's students and up to AUD 30,000 for PhD students. To be eligible, you must be a refugee student in Turkey, have a valid visa, and meet the academic requirements of the chosen university. The deadline for application is 15 September 2026. For more information, visit https://www.australiaawards.gov.au/endeavour.

John Allwright Fellowship

The John Allwright Fellowship covers up to AUD 30,000 per year for master's students and up to AUD 40,000 per year for PhD students. To be eligible, you must be a refugee student in Turkey, have a valid visa, and meet the academic requirements of the chosen university. The deadline for application is 30 November 2026. For more information, visit https://www.aciar.gov.au/fellowships.

Application Steps

To apply for the Australian Awards, follow these steps:

  • Check your eligibility and choose the correct scholarship.

  • Gather all required documents, including your passport, visa, academic transcripts, and language proficiency test results.

  • Create an account on the online application portal.

  • Fill out the application form and upload all required documents.

  • Submit your application before the deadline.

  • Wait for the application to be processed and reviewed.

  • Receive an email with the outcome of your application.

  • If successful, accept the scholarship offer and begin the visa application process.

Documents Checklist

Here is a list of documents you may need to provide:

* Passport

* Visa

* Academic transcripts

* Language proficiency test results (e.g. IELTS or TOEFL)

* Proof of English language proficiency

* Proof of financial support

* Medical certificate

* Police certificate

Frequently Asked Questions

  • Q: What is the Australian Awards program?

A: The Australian Awards program is a scholarship program offered by the Australian government to students from different backgrounds, including refugee students in Turkey, to study in Australia. The program provides opportunities for students to pursue their undergraduate or master's degree in Australia. For more information, visit the official website.

  • Q: How do I apply for the Australian Awards?

A: To apply for the Australian Awards, you need to create an account on the online application portal, fill out the application form, and upload all required documents. The application process typically takes several months, so it's essential to plan ahead and submit your application before the deadline.

  • Q: What are the eligibility criteria for the Australian Awards?

A: The eligibility criteria for the Australian Awards include being a refugee student in Turkey, having a valid visa, and meeting the academic requirements of the chosen university. You must also demonstrate English language proficiency and provide proof of financial support.

  • Q: How much does the Australian Awards cover?

A: The Australian Awards cover up to 100% of tuition fees and living expenses, which can range from AUD 25,000 to AUD 40,000 per year, depending on the scholarship. The exact amount of coverage will depend on the specific scholarship you are awarded.

  • Q: Can I apply for multiple scholarships?

A: Yes, you can apply for multiple scholarships, but you can only receive one scholarship. It's essential to review the eligibility criteria and application requirements for each scholarship before applying.

  • Q: How long does it take to process the application?

A: The application process typically takes several months, so it's essential to plan ahead and submit your application before the deadline. You can check the status of your application online or contact the scholarship provider for updates.

  • Q: Can I bring my family with me to Australia?

A: Yes, you can bring your family with you to Australia, but you will need to meet the requirements for family sponsorship. You must demonstrate that you can support your family financially and provide suitable accommodation.

  • Q: What happens if my application is unsuccessful?

A: If your application is unsuccessful, you can reapply for the next intake. You can also seek feedback from the scholarship provider to improve your application for the next round.

External Resources

For more information on the Australian Awards, visit the Australian Government Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade website or the Australian Government Department of Education, Skills and Employment website.

You can find more information about scholarship opportunities and application processes on our blog at https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.
